name: Calaceite - circular through the lands of Matarraña
name_original: Calaceite - circular por las tierras de Matarraña
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Torre Del Compte are set within the Matarraña region of Teruel, Aragon, Spain. The area is characterized by the Matarraña river, which carves out fertile plains, contrasting with rolling hills and distant views of the Beceite mountains. This diverse landscape includes forests and offers "lonely roads" with ascents reaching over 1,000 meters, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Torre Del Compte The most popular road cycling route is Calaceite - circular through the lands of Matarraña, a moderate 30.7 miles (49.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the Matarraña region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Fuentespalda – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda, a difficult 39.4 miles (63.4 km) path. It features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Valderrobres Stone Bridge – Riverside of Valderrobres loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda, a 37.8 miles (60.9 km) trail leading through riverside areas and challenging terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Torre Del Compte is defined by riverside plains, rolling hills, and distant mountain views. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Torre Del Compte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torre Del Compte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Torre Del Compte?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Torre Del Compte, offering a wide range of options for cyclists. These routes are set within the scenic Matarraña region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Torre Del Compte?
A: The terrain around Torre Del Compte is diverse, featuring picturesque riverside plains along the Matarraña river, rolling hills, and distant views of the Beceite mountains. You'll find 'lonely roads' that traverse forests and offer ascents reaching over 1,000 meters, providing varied and often challenging rides.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists in Torre Del Compte?
A: Yes, while many routes offer significant challenges, there are options for various ability levels. You can find at least one easy route and 15 moderate routes, alongside 22 difficult ones, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What are some options for longer road cycling tours in the Matarraña region?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Valldeltormo Station – Salamanca Tower loop from Antigua Estación de Torre del Compte&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with significant elevation gain. The region is also known for long-distance bikepacking routes connecting towns like Nonaspe, Valderrobres, Beceite, and Arnés.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling around Torre Del Compte?
A: Road cycling routes in the area offer views of the Matarraña river valley and the Beceite mountains. You might also encounter historical landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4364668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic path.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting near Torre Del Compte?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970245628/calaceite-circular-through-the-lands-of-matarrana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calaceite - circular through the lands of Matarraña&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 49.4 km route that explores the local landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Torre Del Compte?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from riverside plains to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to ride on 'lonely roads' through scenic forests and rolling hills.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Torre Del Compte?
A: The Matarraña region generally offers favorable conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are cooler but can still be suitable for cycling on clear days.

Q: Are there services for cyclists, such as bike rentals or repairs, in the Torre Del Compte area?
A: Yes, the Matarraña region is very cyclist-friendly. Local establishments often cater specifically to cyclists, offering services such as bike rentals, repair services, route access information, and even luggage transfer for multi-stage tours.

Q: What historical sites can I visit in Torre Del Compte itself?
A: Beyond cycling, Torre Del Compte offers cultural and historical points of interest. You can visit the 14th-century &lt;strong&gt;Iglesia Parroquial de San Pedro Mártir&lt;/strong&gt;, the 16th-century Renaissance &lt;strong&gt;Town Hall&lt;/strong&gt;, or the sole remaining gate from the town's walled complex, &lt;strong&gt;Portal de San Roque&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Ermita de San Juan&lt;/strong&gt;, a Gothic-Renaissance hermitage, is also located on the outskirts of the village.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentespalda – Valderrobres Stone Bridge loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 63.4 km path featuring over 1,000 meters of elevation gain through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find accommodation that is particularly bike-friendly in the Matarraña region?
A: Yes, the Matarraña region is well-equipped for cyclists. Many accommodations, including hotels and rural houses, specifically cater to cyclists, often providing secure bike storage, washing facilities, and sometimes even packed lunches or route advice.
tips: Beautiful town of Matarraña, a few kilometers from the Catalan border that was declared a Historic-Artistic Site in 1973. Along all the streets weave the routes where we will visit the town, through its beautiful streets.

La Vall de Tormo is a municipality in Matarranya, in the province of Teruel. Throughout the region, this municipality is known as La Vall, without the addition of Tormo. The town is built in a valley, known locally as the River Valley

LaFresneda is a town located on the side of a hill dominated by an old castle where some walls and a tower still remain, called Castillo de la Fresneda. There is also a hermitage, that of Santa Bárbera.
